title: Statusline per-model token/cost segment (main session + subagent transcripts)
action: Add .claude/statusline-usage.mjs: reads the statusline stdin JSON, parses the session's main transcript JSONL plus <session-dir>/subagents/*.jsonl, dedupes assistant messages by message.id (last occurrence wins — streaming appends cumulative usage lines), aggregates per model {input, output, cache_creation 5m/1h, cache_read}, prices them (fable 10/50, opus 5/25, sonnet-5 2/10 intro, sonnet 3/15, haiku 1/5 per MTok; cache write 1.25x/2x input, read 0.1x), and prints a compact segment like 'fable 5.2M/$3.10 + sonnet 1.1M/$0.42 = $3.52'. Signature cache (file sizes+mtimes) in os.tmpdir() so unchanged refreshes skip re-parsing. Fail-open: any error prints nothing, exit 0. Then append the segment to .claude/statusline-command.sh via node, guarded so a missing node or script failure never breaks the existing line.

truths: Subagent transcripts under <session-dir>/subagents/ are included, so models other than the main session model appear with their own token totals and cost
Duplicate JSONL lines for one message id are not double-counted
Script failure or missing node leaves the existing statusline output intact (fail-open)
node --check passes on the .mjs and bash -n passes on the .sh
Smoke test against a real session transcript with subagents prints a per-model segment
prohibitions: No changes outside the two declared files
Never exit non-zero from the usage script (statusline must not break)
